_about: Grounded feature-combination -> pattern test cases for the DIAGNOSIS MAPPING (not feature detection). Each case is a canonical tongue picture from TCM tongue-diagnosis references; `accept` lists the pattern id(s) that reference sources consider correct for that combination. Runner: evaluation/eval_mapping.py. Sources: Sacred Lotus TCM tongue diagnosis; Maciocia, Tongue Diagnosis in Chinese Medicine; Delphi consensus PMC8983216; Wang Qi CCMQ 9-constitution model.

cases.note: pale body + tooth marks + thin white coat = Spleen Qi (→Qi-deficiency constitution)
pale + swollen + WET, thin white (non-greasy) coat = Spleen/Kidney Yang deficiency; the wet sheen is what separates it from damp-heat
red body + greasy yellow coat = Damp-Heat
thick greasy white coat + swollen, normal body = Phlegm-Dampness
red body + peeled/no coat + cracks = Yin deficiency (empty heat)
red dots/prickles + yellow greasy coat = Heat in blood / Damp-Heat
pale + thin (not swollen) body = Blood deficiency (under-nourished)
light-red body + thin white coat, no marks = Balanced
HARD/contradictory: greasy yellow coat (damp) + cracks (yin-dry) = Damp-Heat consuming fluids (or damp-heat + yin-def)
red tip (Heart/upper-jiao heat) on an otherwise near-normal tongue + scanty coat = heat, leans Yin-deficiency / Heart-heat
CONTEXT FLIP: same swelling as the yang case, but with a RED body + greasy YELLOW coat = Damp-Heat (proves swelling flips meaning by body colour/coat)
